sections.paragraph:
(“the court of death”), [Hazarmaveth], pr.n. of a district in Arabia Felix, situated on the Indian sea, abounding in frankincense, myrrh, and aloes; but remarkable on account of the unhealthiness of the Climate (whence its name); it is still called by the Arabs حَضْرَمَوْتُ, حَضْرُمَوْتِ Gen 10:26. See Abulfedæ Arabia, edit. Gagn. page 45; Niebuhr’s Description of Arabia, page 283-294.

[חֲצֹרֵר (כ׳) Piel part. 2Ch 5:12 see חֲצֹצֵר .]
